findings.issue: Unprotected JSON.parse on file contents — corrupted _ai_memories.json will crash the app on startup with no recovery path
findings.recommendation: Wrap JSON.parse in try/catch, log warning and skip corrupted file rather than crashing

findings.issue: Unprotected JSON.parse on database row — corrupted plugin settings will crash PluginSettingsManager.load() with no catch
findings.recommendation: Wrap JSON.parse in try/catch, return empty object on parse failure and log a warning
findings.code_snippet: const settings: Record<string, unknown> = row ? JSON.parse(row.settings) : {};

findings.issue: Three independent copies of message restoration logic (JSON.parse toolCalls, filter system messages, reconstruct ChatMessage[]) — divergence risk is high since any fix must be applied in 3 places
findings.recommendation: Extract a shared `deserializeChatMessages(rows)` utility into src/ai/chat.ts or a new src/ai/message-utils.ts, import from all three consumers

findings.issue: Infinite retry loop with no backoff or attempt limit — if resolveBuiltinComponent fails or returns null permanently, this retries every 1 second forever, creating unnecessary load
findings.recommendation: Add exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s...) and a maximum retry count (e.g., 10 attempts), then show an error state

findings.issue: Event listener leak — adds listener via addEventListener('change', () => ...) but cleanup uses `permStatus.onchange = null`, which only removes handlers set via the onchange property, not addEventListener
findings.recommendation: Store the handler function in a variable and use removeEventListener('change', handler) in cleanup, or use onchange = handler instead of addEventListener

findings.issue: ThemeManager registers a 'change' event listener on MediaQueryList in the constructor but never removes it — since it's a module-level singleton this is a minor leak, but prevents proper teardown in tests
findings.recommendation: Add a destroy() method that calls removeEventListener, or store the handler reference for cleanup

findings.issue: Unsafe type assertion `(storageInfo as { mode: string }).mode` — the catch fallback returns `{ mode: 'unknown' }` as a plain object but the assertion assumes it always has the full shape, bypassing type safety
findings.recommendation: Define an explicit return type for the Promise.all result or use a type guard to check mode property existence

findings.issue: Explicit `any` type annotation in message restoration array — `toolCalls?: any` bypasses type checking for tool call data in the frontend session switch path
findings.recommendation: Use the proper ToolCall type from ai/types.ts instead of any

findings.issue: Plugin instance accessed via `as any` to reach timeblocking store — tightly couples the API layer to plugin internals without type safety
findings.recommendation: Define a typed interface for plugin instances that expose a store (e.g., `PluginWithStore`) or use the plugin API's getStore() method

findings.issue: Readable.fromWeb(res.body as any) uses `as any` cast — Node.js ReadableStream vs Web ReadableStream type mismatch
findings.recommendation: Use a proper type assertion or conditional check: `Readable.fromWeb(res.body as ReadableStream<Uint8Array>)` if on Node 18+

findings.issue: Settings load failure silently caught with no error logging — if the API is unreachable, user gets default settings with no indication that saved preferences were lost
findings.recommendation: Add console.warn logging in the catch block to indicate settings load failure

findings.issue: Two settings sections silently swallow load errors with `.catch(() => setLoaded(true))` — user won't know if their AI settings failed to load
findings.recommendation: Add console.warn for failed settings loads so debugging is possible

findings.issue: Model fetch failure silently caught — sets modelsFailed flag but the catch block has no logging, making it hard to diagnose why models fail to load
findings.recommendation: Add console.warn in the catch to log the actual error for debugging

findings.issue: Session restore try/catch silently drops errors with only a comment '// Non-critical' — if ChatSession creation fails, the user gets an empty session with no feedback about what went wrong
findings.recommendation: Log the error with logger.warn to enable debugging of session restore failures

findings.issue: voice.speak() error silently swallowed — if TTS fails, the user gets no feedback that auto-speak didn't work
findings.recommendation: Add console.warn to log TTS failures for debugging

findings.issue: Plugin content polling at 500ms/1000ms intervals is aggressive — creates continuous network/API load for any open plugin view even when nothing changes
findings.recommendation: Consider using server-sent events (SSE) or a longer polling interval with a manual refresh button. At minimum, add a visibility check to pause polling when tab is hidden

findings.issue: 4 eslint-disable directives for @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any in 55 lines — this bridge module has the highest suppression density in the codebase
findings.recommendation: Define a PluginComponentType = React.FC<Record<string, unknown>> and use it throughout instead of any

findings.issue: Duplicated task extraction regex patterns (LIST_PREFIX and ACTION_VERBS) maintained independently in both files with a comment to stay in sync — fragile manual synchronization
findings.recommendation: Extract shared constants to src/utils/task-extraction.ts and import from both consumers

findings.issue: (window as any).SpeechRecognition — Web Speech API types available via @types/dom-speech-recognition but not installed (carried over from round 1, still unresolved)
findings.recommendation: Install @types/dom-speech-recognition or add a local type declaration to eliminate 3 `as any` casts

findings.issue: 4 `as any` casts for piper-tts library API — library types incomplete (carried over from round 1, still unresolved)
findings.recommendation: Create a local piper-tts.d.ts type declaration file

findings.issue: ErrorBoundary only wraps the main view area — Sidebar, AIChatPanel, and TaskDetailPanel are unprotected; a crash in any of these takes down the entire app (carried over from round 1, still unresolved)
findings.recommendation: Wrap Sidebar, AIChatPanel, and TaskDetailPanel each in their own ErrorBoundary

findings.issue: Color value cast via `as any` — `DEFAULT_PROJECT_COLORS.includes(color as any)` used to work around TypeScript's strict array.includes type checking
findings.recommendation: Use a type-safe alternative: `(DEFAULT_PROJECT_COLORS as readonly string[]).includes(color)` or define a type guard

findings.issue: .swarm/ directory is not gitignored — swarm audit reports will be committed to the repository
findings.recommendation: Add `.swarm/` to .gitignore if these are intended to be ephemeral audit artifacts, or keep as-is if they should be tracked

findings.issue: Stale TODO comment '// TODO: Start UI or CLI based on context' has been present since early development — entry point logic is handled elsewhere now
findings.recommendation: Remove the TODO or implement the UI/CLI branching logic if still planned
findings.code_snippet: // TODO: Start UI or CLI based on context

findings.issue: Three separate API calls with individual .catch(() => fallback) in Promise.all — if any fail, the About page shows partial/misleading data (e.g., 0 tasks) without indicating an error occurred
findings.recommendation: Track error state and show 'unavailable' instead of '0' when an API call fails
